description: Need to pull all the service names from /home/user/infra/services.json where status is "degraded" — just the names, one per line. jq is installed, should be straightforward.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/infra/services.json exists and contains valid JSON:
```json
{
  "services": [
    {"name": "auth-api", "status": "healthy", "port": 8080},
    {"name": "payment-gateway", "status": "degraded", "port": 8081},
    {"name": "user-db", "status": "healthy", "port": 5432},
    {"name": "cache-redis", "status": "degraded", "port": 6379},
    {"name": "notification-svc", "status": "healthy", "port": 8082},
    {"name": "inventory-api", "status": "degraded", "port": 8083}
  ]
}
```
- jq is installed and available in PATH.
- /home/user/infra is readable by the agent user.

Expected final state:
- Agent runs a jq command (or equivalent) that outputs exactly:
```
payment-gateway
cache-redis
inventory-api
```
- Three lines, one service name per line, no quotes, no extra whitespace, in the order they appear in the file.

Invariants:
- /home/user/infra/services.json remains unchanged.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The solution must actually filter by status="degraded", not hardcode the three names. If the JSON were modified to change which services are degraded, the same command should produce the correct updated list.
name: task_000000_9bc868cd
